steps.title: Spot the key signal
steps.content: The blank is the subject of the sentence: "______ of this approach cite the production of less material waste and a faster return on investment." Whatever the blank is, it names a group of people who cite benefits in order to support the modular construction approach. The word must mean advocates or supporters.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The word needs to name people who actively support and promote an approach. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Components: means parts or elements of a system — components are not people and cannot "cite" anything. ✗
  • Choice B — Safeguards: means protective measures — safeguards are not people; they cannot cite benefits to support an approach. ✗
  • Choice C — Proponents: means people who advocate for something — proponents of modular construction would naturally cite its benefits (less waste, faster ROI) to support it. ✓
  • Choice D — Epitomes: means perfect examples of something — an epitome of an approach cannot itself cite evidence; this is not a category of people who argue for the method. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
